summary: Deprecated Count method is still in the public API
description: The CRUD API still includes "Count", "CountDocuments" and "EstimatedDocumentCount", but Count is deprecated in the CRUD API with a note that new drivers shouldn't implement it.  So we still have a chance to remove it before GA to avoid confusing users with three similar sounding methods.

summary: BSON field '$changeStream.maxAwaitTimeMS' is an unknown field
description:
I could be doing something wrong, but it appears that the maxAwaitTimeMS on a change stream is not always getting converted to maxTimeMs before being sent on the wire.  
{code:java}
var stream *mongo.ChangeStream
 opts := options.ChangeStream()
 opts.SetMaxAwaitTime(time.Duration(10) * time.Second)
 opts.SetFullDocument(options.UpdateLookup)
 opts.SetStartAtOperationTime(startAt)
 c := client.Database("test").Collection("test")
 stream, err = c.Watch(context.Background(), pipeline, opts){code}
With SetMaxAwaitTime applied I get the following error: 

BSON field '$changeStream.maxAwaitTimeMS' is an unknown field

summary: client.Disconnect(nil) causes a panic
Can only interact with GridFS files by id via ObjectIds
description: GridFS files collection files can have an {{_id}} of any type, but the GridFS function signatures that accept an id only accept {{ObjectIds}} (e.g. [{{bucket.OpenUploadStreamWithId}}|https://godoc.org/github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/mongo/gridfs#Bucket.OpenUploadStreamWithID]). This should be expanded to accept any valid BSON type.

summary: Server String() function access unprotected variables
description: The Server type's String function accesses variables from the struct which can be updated asynchronously. Fields like averageRTT should be taken from the immutable description rather than the server struct itself. connectionstate should be changed to use the atomic package so it can be loaded/stored without race conditions.

summary: GSSAPI Authentication starts SASL conversation wrong
Remove all public use of 'x' packages in the options package
Allow TLSConfig as a client option
description: Use a TLSConfig in the SetSSL() function in clientoptions.go instead of SSLOpt. This requires changing WithConnString() and adding WithTLSConfig() in topology_options.go.

summary: Clean up mongo.newClient
description: The {{mongo.newClient}} function has been through many options design iterations and has some cruft left over from previous designs. Specifically, it handles overriding the {{ReadPreference}} and other types when they are nil, including reading them from the connection string. We should likely just be checking to see if they are nil and then adding the defaults, not read them from the connstring, which is handled by {{options.MergeClientOptions}}.

summary: GridFS default chunk size is 255 kB, not 255 KiB
description: The GridFS specification defines the default chunk size as 255KB, which is ambiguous. All the other drivers seem to have interpreted it as 255KiB, whereas the go driver defines it as 255kB. This should be fixed to align with the other drivers' behavior.

summary: Create mongo.CommandError to wrap command.Error
description: The {{command.Error}} type is accessed by users when they need to do manual retrying of transactions. This type is currently within the {{x/}} directory and is likely to change or be moved in the future. As part of GODRIVER-793, wrap instances of {{command.Error}} with a newly created {{mongo.CommandError}}. This new error should have all the same fields and functionality of {{command.Error}}.

summary: TransformDocument should document how it handles nil
description: nil is equivalent to an empty document, but this is not documented.

summary: GoDoc for mongo.Client does not match API
description: The changes from GODRIVER-793 didn't include updates to the documentation. Update the README and the {{mongo/doc.go}} documentation with the new API.

summary: Unable to authenticate to a replica set member using the keyfile
description:
This is a regression introduced by GODRIVER-803.  The commit before that one does not have this problem.

 

How to reproduce:

 
 # Start a 1-node replica set with auth and a keyfile containing "testkeyfilecontents" 
 # Run the attached go-driver-auth.go program.  The following happens

 
{noformat}
[tim@jazzstep-2 tst]$ go run go-driver-auth.go

goroutine 1 [running]:
main.main()
	/Users/tim/tst/go-driver-auth.go:38 +0x520
exit status 2
{noformat}

summary: Alternative Import Path
description:
Currently the Go driver's import path is {{github.com/10gen/mongo-go-driver}}. While we currently use GitHub as the canonical repository we might not want to keep it that way forever. Go supports remote import paths that aren't known source code hosting services, in fact mgo currently uses one (gopkg.in).

This raises two questions:

* Should we create an alternative import for the new Go driver?
* If so, what should that import path be?

For reference, the rules around go gettable import paths are here: https://golang.org/cmd/go/#hdr-Remote_import_paths

summary: Docs out of date for how to connect
bson can not marshal datetime ISODate json correct
description:
fix datetime marshal json format. refer to rfc3339

in golang 's json, the "time" format should be as same at rfc3339

 

before:

```

{

    "created_at" : 1551093564256,

}

```

```

{

    "after" : "2019-02-25T11:19:24.256Z",

}

```

more: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/pull/131

summary: Chunks collection files_id_1_n_1 index not unique
description:
According to the GridFS [spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst#indexes], the {{\{files_id: 1, n: 1\}}} index on the chunks collection is supposed to be unique, but the go driver creates it without specifying that.

This allows orphan chunks to be made when writing a file with an _id that is already present.

summary: zLib compression does not work
description:
It looks like zLib compression is broken.

Running the tests with `MONGO_GO_DRIVER_COMPRESSOR=zlib` causes failures. To recreate this first set up a mongod with `--networkMessageCompressors="zlib"`. Many tests fail, but here is one example:
{code:java}TOPOLOGY=server MONGO_GO_DRIVER_COMPRESSOR=zlib go test -v ./x/network/integration -run TestCompression{code}
And the result:
{code:java}=== RUN   TestCompression
--- FAIL: TestCompression (0.01s)
        Error Trace:    config.go:192
                        once.go:44
                        config.go:175
                        compressor_test.go:30
        Error:          Received unexpected error:
                        connection(localhost:27017[-2]) unable to decode message length: EOF
FAIL
FAIL    github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/x/network/integration        0.029s
{code}
I believe there are three underlying problems with the zLib compression.

Firstly, `(z *ZlibCompressor) CompressBytes()` will always return an empty slice. This is because the `dest` slice is copied into a writer as the `buf` value. This `buf` is resliced as it is written to by the zLib compressor. So although the underlying array of dest is being updated, `dest`'s length is not being updated. So returning `dest` just returns a slice of zero elements. 

Secondly, `(z *ZlibCompressor) UncompressBytes()` will also always return an empty slice. `io.ReadFull(zlibReader, dest)` reads exactly `len(dest)` bytes from `zlibReader` into `dest`. Since `c.uncompressBuf` is passed into `UncompressBytes` as a zero element slice, zlibReader will never read anything into dest. See [connection.go|#L294] for context.

Thirdly, the way compressors are initialized leads to race conditions for the zLib compressor. Currently, a single compressor is initialized by `topology.WithConnString()` and is used for every connection (see [topology_options.go|#L196]). This is fine for Snappy. But for zLib, if multiple connections use the same zLib writer at the same time, they will all be accessing and modifying the writer's internal buffers. I think initializing a new compressor per connection is probably the way to go.

I'm submitting a PR to address these three issues. Would love some more eyes to ensure I haven't misunderstood anything here.

summary: Retryable Writes is broken for batch splits
description: When a retryable error happens during a write operation that requires batch splitting the entire operation is retried instead of just the failed batch and subsequent batches.

summary: ObjectID should be quoted in bsoncore.Value's String() method
description: In x/bsonx/bsoncore/value.go, the function {{func (v Value) String() string}} writes ObjectedIDs as {{{"$oid":%s}}}, but the %s should be quoted, like this: {{{"$oid":"%s"}}}.

summary: Panic when passing non-ObjectID file ID to bucket.OpenDownloadStream
description: Due to the hard cast at [bucket.go:354|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/blob/master/mongo/gridfs/bucket.go#L354], the driver will panic if a non-ObjectId is passed in for the fileID when calling bucket.OpenDownloadStream. Removing the cast seems to resolve the issue.

summary: Panic when passing in UploadOptions with Metadata but no Registry to bucket.OpenUploadStream
description:
Passing in an {{UploadOptions}} specifying {{Metadata}} but not {{Registry}} to {{bucket.OpenUploadStream}} causes the driver to panic due to a nil pointer dereference.

This is due to the {{options.GridFSUpload}} [constructor|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/blob/master/mongo/options/gridfsoptions.go#L114] not setting the default registry as the [docstring|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/blob/master/mongo/options/gridfsoptions.go#L109] suggests, and the lack of a nil check in {{parseUploadOptions}}, where the panic actually occurs.

summary: bsonrw.valueReader skip does not properly skip regular expressions
description: When skipping a regular expression, the null byte for options is not found properly.

summary: Cannot query when having a regular expression in MongoDB Document
Server string representation typo: Avergage RTT
description: The Server String() method returns an "Avergage RTT" instead of an "Average RTT" (extra g).

summary: Go driver cannot decode gridfs files collection documents if the length field is not stored as int64
description:
From the gridfs spec:
{quote}
Note: drivers SHOULD store length as Int64 and chunkSize as Int32 when creating new GridFS files. However, drivers MUST be able to handle existing GridFS files where the length and chunkSize fields might have been stored using a different numeric data type.
{quote}

However, the Go driver cannot handle existing GridFS files if the type of length (or chunkSize, presumably) is not {{int64}}.

To reproduce, insert some GridFS data using the python driver (which stores length as {{Int32}}) and try to read it with the Go driver. It will panic with output similar to this:
{code}
panic: Call of bsoncore.Value.Int64 on 32-bit integer type

goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/mongodb/mongo-tools/vendor/github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/x/bsonx/bsoncore.Value.Int64(0x110, 0xc000300094, 0x4, 0x10c, 0x107)
        /Users/patrickfreed/go/src/github.com/mongodb/mongo-tools/vendor/github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/x/bsonx/bsoncore/value.go:693 +0x18a
github.com/mongodb/mongo-tools/vendor/github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/bson.RawValue.Int64(0x10, 0xc000300094, 0x4, 0x10c, 0x0, 0x1)
        /Users/patrickfreed/go/src/github.com/mongodb/mongo-tools/vendor/github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/bson/raw_value.go:273 +0x66
github.com/mongodb/mongo-tools/vendor/github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/mongo/gridfs.(*Bucket).openDownloadStream(0xc000278000, 0x1526180, 0xc000238480, 0xc000107a38, 0x1, 0x1, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0)
        /Users/patrickfreed/go/src/github.com/mongodb/mongo-tools/vendor/github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/mongo/gridfs/bucket.go:341 +0x347
github.com/mongodb/mongo-tools/vendor/github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/mongo/gridfs.(*Bucket).OpenDownloadStreamByName(0xc000278000, 0x7ffeefbff6e0, 0xc, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x23, 0xc000107cf0, 0x1)
        /Users/patrickfreed/go/src/github.com/mongodb/mongo-tools/vendor/github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/mongo/gridfs/bucket.go:214 +0x41f
{code}

summary: panic when calling Watch on a Client that has not been connected
description:
With

{code}
	client, _ := mongo.NewClient(options.Client().ApplyURI("mongodb://localhost:27017"))

	// Oops, forgot to call client.Connect!

	_, _ = client.Watch(context.Background(), []bson.D{})
{code}

the driver panics with

{noformat}
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x0 pc=0x11ee797]

summary: options.MergeClientOptions doesn't handle errors properly
description:
When merging a set of {{options.ClientOptions}} we do not ensure that we copy the error from a given {{options.ClientOptions}} which means that errors from {{ApplyURI}} are suppressed.

To fix this, check if the {{err}} field of an {{options.ClientOptions}} instance is non-nil and if so, immediately return the {{options.ClientOptions}} with the error before merging.

summary: Problem if filter in Distinct is nil
Topology should synchronously update ServerDescriptions
description: An unknown server can be selected by SelectServer if the goroutine to update the topology copy of the server description does not run before server selection runs.

summary: Document Docker DNS issue
description: See GODRIVER-829.  While we don't currently consider this a driver bug, it has been affecting users and we should document it somewhere so that it's easier for people to resolve (no pun intended) the issue themselves.

summary: BSON Marshaller ignores fields of an embedded structure of the schema
Default for timestamps is a zero'd *time.Location, instead of `nil`
description:
We are currently in the process of migrating away from `mgo`, and to the official driver (thank you for making it!).

In order to perform the migration smoothly, we are writing a suite of parity integration tests, verifying the exact behavior differences between the two libraries. (e.g. reflect.DeepEqual on two objects, one returned from `mgo` the other from official)

One of our parity tests is failing, in regards to time zone translation when marshaling/un-marshaling a Golang `time.Time`.  `mgo` sets the internal `*time.Location` as `nil`, while the official driver sets it to a zero value.

I believe `nil` is actually more correct, as this mirrors what the standard library does. See `time.go#192`. When the time is set to `UTC`, it actually sets `loc` to `nil`.

Because `time.Location` is set to the zero value, this is the representation of the time to default to system location, rather than UTC.

summary: GoDoc out of date for cursor example
description: [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/blob/master/mongo/doc.go#L38] uses the DeocdeBytes method, which was removed in the cursor redesign. Change this to use Decode instead.

summary: Legacy Find is not respecting limit
description:
STR: 
  The client should be connecting to a mongo instance such that
{code:java}
 top.SelectServer.Description.WireVersion.Max < 4{code}
Create and execute a query that specifies limit.

Observe that a multiple of batchSize documents are returned, not the value of limit.

 * if limit < batchSize, batchSize is returned

 * if limit >= batchSize, n * batchSize is returned

Notes:
 After a lengthy debugging session, we discovered the following. Hopefully it will guide you to a solution.
 * BatchCursor.Next() calls  legacyGetMore, which appends batchSize documents to currentBatch.Data
 * If limit < batchSize, numToReturn is a negative number. This may or may not be causing problems. Hard for us to tell as outsiders. This should probably be a short circuit condition
 * An extra network request is made. This causes more documents to be added to bc.currentBatch.Data. Limit is not being honored, as there is no logic similar to NewLegacyBatchCursor #118-#126 in legacyGetMore.

summary: Improvement Upsert in FindAndModify
description:
WHY:

 - I'm integrating mongo-go-driver into our project to replace the mgo library. At the command findAndModify, I want to get the UpdatedExisting field in FindAndModify struct when using the Upsert option. That will let me know the document is inserted or updated. For example: FindOneAndUpdate function.

summary: Filed Lenght
bulk write doesn't report write concern error
description: collection.BulkWrite doesn't report write concern errors. I think the problem is in driverlegacy/bulk_write.go:runBatch, which returns a BulkWriteException for each batch. This exception captures the write errors for each write, but not the write concern error.

summary: options.FindOptions.SetLimit() docs suggest negative limits are still supported, but they're not
description:
The [docs for {{options.FindOptions.SetLimit()}}|https://godoc.org/go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/mongo/options#FindOptions.SetLimit] suggest that negative limits are valid, when in fact the server returns an error if you try ({{"Error: (BadValue) Limit value must be non-negative, but received: <N>"}}).  This is because the driver uses the newer {{find}} command, which doesn't support negative limits.  

({{mgo}} supported negative limits, presumably by converting it to a positive limit + {{singleBatch=true}}, which adds to the confusion - as does the fact that the shell's {{cursor.limit()}} supports negative limits too!)

summary: Support wildcardProjection index option
description: The driver doesn't support the wildcardProjection index option which should be available for MongoDB 4.1.8

summary: extjson date parser doesn't work for dates after the year 2262
Upgrade testing to go 1.12
description: We are still using 1.11 to test on Evergreen. Upgrade to use go 1.12.

summary: SingleResult.Decode only works once
Decoding SingleResult or cursor to nil pointer type panics
description:
Decoding to a nil pointer of a type panics.

This pattern works:
{noformat}struct X {}
var x X
res.Decode(&x)
{noformat}
This pattern panics:
{noformat}var x *X
res.Decode(x)
{noformat}
This is subtle and users are likely to mess this up. We should detect this condition and return an instructive error instead of panicking and should document this limitation clearly.

We do this for other usage errors, such as "argument to Decode must be a pointer to a type, but got map[]".

Sample program:
{noformat}package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log"

	"go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/bson"
	"go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/bson/primitive"
	"go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/mongo"
)

type X struct {
	ID   primitive.ObjectID `bson:"_id,omitempty"`
	Name string             `bson:"name"`
}

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()

	client, err := mongo.Connect(ctx)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	coll := client.Database("test").Collection("decodeit")
	_ = coll.Drop(ctx)

	doc := X{Name: "hello world"}
	res, err := coll.InsertOne(ctx, doc)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	id := res.InsertedID
	fmt.Println("Inserted", id)

	got := coll.FindOne(ctx, bson.D{{"_id", id}})
	if got.Err() != nil {
		log.Fatal(got.Err())
	}

	var doc2 *X
	err = got.Decode(doc2)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("doc2 %v", err)
	}
	fmt.Println("doc2", *doc2)
}
{noformat}
Output:
{noformat}$ go run main.go
Inserted ObjectID("5cc65cd9731bafcc96ccb4f3")
panic: reflect: call of reflect.Value.Type on zero Value

goroutine 1 [running]:
reflect.Value.Type(0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0)
        /usr/local/go/src/reflect/value.go:1813 +0x169
go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/bson.(*Decoder).Decode(0xc000083cb0, 0x140ce60, 0x0, 0x14567e0, 0xc000083cb0)
        /Users/david/golang/pkg/mod/go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver@v1.0.1/bson/decoder.go:82 +0x25d
go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/bson.unmarshalFromReader(0xc0000c2070, 0xc00005e400, 0x0, 0x0, 0x15f6ae0, 0xc00005e480, 0x140ce60, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0)
        /Users/david/golang/pkg/mod/go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver@v1.0.1/bson/unmarshal.go:100 +0xff
go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/bson.UnmarshalWithRegistry(0xc0000c2070, 0xc0000ce223, 0x2c, 0x5d, 0x140ce60, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0)
        /Users/david/golang/pkg/mod/go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver@v1.0.1/bson/unmarshal.go:45 +0x10a
go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/mongo.(*Cursor).Decode(...)
        /Users/david/golang/pkg/mod/go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver@v1.0.1/mongo/cursor.go:123
go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/mongo.(*SingleResult).Decode(0xc00005e440, 0x140ce60, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0)
        /Users/david/golang/pkg/mod/go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver@v1.0.1/mongo/single_result.go:59 +0x197
main.main()
        /Users/david/tmp/godriver-decode-pointer/main.go:43 +0x426

summary: Where should we retrieve a description.Server from during dispatching?
description:
During [this code review comment|https://review.gerrithub.io/c/403372/18/mongo/private/roots/dispatch/aggregate.go#60], the question of where we should retrieve the server description from came up.

The question is: Should we store the required components of a description.Server on a connection and only use those required components in the commands or should we continue to use the description attached a topology.Server?

summary: record codeName for WriteConcernErrors
description: When we get a writeConcernError from the server, we don't record the codeName.

summary: Remove topology.Topology.registry field
description: After GODRIVER-3 was completed, the {{registry}} field of {{topology.Server}} was left behind, even though it is no longer used. Remove it along with the {{topology.WithRegistry}} function that configures that field.
